Summary190: After years of grief a Nigerian-Ghanaian family celebrates their 12-year-old quadriplegic sons recovery but joy turns dark as he desires his sister and her boyfriends vanish.
Summary400: After years of grief a troubled Nigerian Ghanaian family is overjoyed when their 12 year old quadriplegic son Kujo suddenly recovers. But Kujo begins developing forbidden feelings for his older sister Chioma. Despite intervention from their mother and a powerful priest Kujo stays obsessed. Soon Chiomas boyfriends start vanishing one by one. The familys joy slowly turns into fear.
Cast:DisplayName: Emmanuel Asamoah;Manuela Ifunanya Ulasi;Brian Angels;David Asare Masterwille

Summary400: A global football documentary exploring the nations, confederations and cultures that shaped the sport—from World Cup legends to modern powerhouses—revealing how politics, academies, leagues and iconic tournaments built football’s biggest stage.

Season 1 explores the World Cup’s greatest icons and defining stories—from Pelé and Maradona to Messi, Mbappé, Cruyff, Zidane and Iniesta—celebrating unforgettable goals, rivalries and tactical brilliance that made football’s biggest stage the ultimate test of greatness.
The 1966 World Cup winners have shaped football culture for over 100 years and the English Premier League remains the most influential domestic competition in the game
Hosts of the 1994 World Cup, the USA's men's national team have often been overshadowed by their female counterparts.
